✦ Open for Reuse

Agent Framework

A reusable, production-ready framework for building conversational AI agents — with streaming web UI, auto tool/skill discovery, and single-config rebranding.

Python Backend React Frontend Claude API WebSocket Streaming Git Submodule

The Core Insight

The Model is the Agent. The Code is the Harness.

An agent is just an LLM in a while loop.
It thinks, acts, observes — and loops until done.
🧠
The LLM (Driver)
Reasons, plans, decides what to do next. This is the intelligence — you don't engineer it, you harness it.
🚗
The Harness (Vehicle)
Tools, knowledge, UI, permissions. Gives the LLM clear perception and precise actions. This is what we build.

The framework's job is simple: give Claude the right tools and knowledge, then get out of the way. No rigid workflows. No decision trees. Just a clean loop.

The Agent Loop

One Loop Powers Everything

Bash is all you need.

User → messages[] → LLM → response │ stop_reason == "tool_use"? ╱ ╲ yes no ↓ ↓ execute tool return text append result (done!) loop back ──→ messages[]

The model decides

Claude chooses when to call tools, which to use, and when to stop. Our code just executes.

Same loop, every agent

FinGuru, a support bot, a code assistant — same loop. Only the tools and knowledge change.

REPL or Web UI

Optional streaming callback. Pass it for WebSocket, omit for terminal. One codebase, two interfaces.

Bash is all you need

Give an LLM bash and it can do almost anything. Domain tools just make it faster and more reliable.

The Harness

What You Plug Into the Loop

🔧
Tools
What the agent can do
+
📚
Skills
What the agent knows
+
🎭
Persona
Who the agent is
+
🖥️
UI
How users interact
=
🤖
Your Agent
Ready to ship

🔧 Tools — Python functions

Write a function with type hints and a docstring. The framework auto-converts it into a tool Claude can call. Query a database, hit an API, generate a chart — anything Python can do.

📚 Skills — Markdown files

Write domain knowledge in Markdown: terminology, rules, which tools to use when. The agent loads skills on-demand to save context space — like an expert opening a reference manual.

🎭 Persona — System prompt

A single Markdown template that defines the agent's name, role, and behavioral rules. Two placeholders get filled automatically: working directory and available skills.

🖥️ UI — One config file

Name, logo, colors, sample questions — change one TypeScript file and you have a completely different product. The React chat UI, WebSocket streaming, and auth are all built in.

What is an Agent Framework?

Everything you need to go from idea to production agent.

🔄

Agent Main Loop

Think → Act → Observe → Repeat

🐚

Bash Tool

The universal capability layer

🔧

Tool Discovery & Execution

Auto-register, dispatch, return results

📚

Skill Loading

On-demand domain knowledge

🖥️

UI

Chat interface with streaming

🔌

API Interface

WebSocket & REST endpoints

💾

Memory System TBI

Context compression & persistence

An agent loop + bash is already a working agent. The framework adds everything else to make it production-grade.

Architecture

How It All Fits Together

my-new-project/
├── framework/           ← git submodule (don't touch)
│   ├── agent/
│   │   ├── app.py          ← Agent loop & streaming
│   │   ├── tool_loader.py  ← Auto-discovers your tools
│   │   ├── skills.py       ← Auto-discovers your skills
│   │   ├── tools.py        ← Built-in tools (bash, file I/O)
│   │   └── todo.py         ← Todo tracking
│   └── web/             ← Shared UI (backend + frontend)
│
├── skills/              ← YOUR domain knowledge
│   └── my-skill/SKILL.md
│
├── tools/               ← YOUR tool functions
│   └── my_tools.py
│
├── prompts/             ← YOUR agent persona
│   └── system.md
│
└── web/                 ← YOUR UI overrides
    ├── project-config.ts   ← Branding, theme, questions
    └── public/             ← Logos, favicon
🧠

Framework (Generic)

Agent loop, tool execution, streaming, context compression, file ops, todo tracking. Shared across all projects.

🔧

Project Layer (You Write)

Skills (domain knowledge), tools (Python functions), system prompt (persona), and UI config (name, logo, colors).

🔌

Auto-Discovery

Drop files in skills/ or tools/ — the framework finds them on startup. No registration needed.

Reuse Guide

Create a New Agent in 3 Steps

1

Define Your Tools tools/

Create tools/my_tools.py with a __tools__ list. Each function's signature becomes a Claude tool schema. Connect to your database, API, or any data source.

__tools__ = ['query_data', 'get_metrics'] def query_data(sql: str) -> str: """Run a SQL query against the data warehouse. Args: sql: SQL query string""" return execute(sql).to_json()
2

Write Your Skills skills/

Create skills/my-skill/SKILL.md with domain knowledge — terminology, rules, available tools, and example workflows. This is the agent's "expert playbook".

3

Set Your Persona prompts/ + Brand project-config.ts

Rewrite prompts/system.md with the agent's new name and instructions. Create web/project-config.ts to override name, logo, colors, and sample questions.

0

Lines of framework code to modify

~3

Files to create for a new domain

1

Config file to rebrand the UI

Summary

What You Get Out of the Box

Capability What It Does You Write
Agent Loop LLM interaction, tool dispatch, parallel execution Nothing
Tool System Auto-converts Python functions → Claude tools Just Python functions
Skill System Two-layer knowledge injection, auto-discovery Just Markdown files
Context Compression 3-layer strategy, transcript archival Nothing
Streaming Web UI WebSocket, React chat, conversation history One config file
Auth & Persistence Azure AD SSO, DB-backed chat history Nothing
Built-in Tools File I/O, bash, todo tracking, subagents Nothing

Focus on your domain. The framework handles everything else.

Get started: git submodule add <framework-repo> framework

Live Example

FinGuru — Built with this Framework

Conversation Sidebar

Search, browse, and resume past conversations. Auto-titled from first message.

Sample Questions

Configurable welcome cards with icons. Defined in project-config.ts — no component edits.

Streaming Chat

Real-time token delivery, inline charts, tool execution indicators. All built in.

Full Rebrand = 1 File

Name, logo, colors, questions — create project-config.ts and you have a completely new product.

Recap

Trust the model.
Engineer the harness.

The loop is universal Tools = Python functions Skills = Markdown files Rebrand = 1 config file Framework code = 0 changes

Write domain knowledge, not infrastructure. The framework handles the rest.

Backend

Agent Core — What the Framework Handles

⚡

Agent Loop

Agent.run(messages, on_event)

Single entry point for both REPL and web UI. Streams via callback or prints to console. Handles tool calls in parallel.

🔧

ToolLoader

Scans tools/*.py for __tools__ list. Converts Python function signatures + docstrings into Claude tool schemas automatically.

Zero boilerplate — just write functions.

📚

SkillLoader

Two-layer system: skill names always visible in system prompt; full body injected on-demand via load_skill tool.

Saves tokens — loads knowledge only when needed.

📦

Context Compression

Auto-compact (summarize at 50k tokens) and manual compact (agent-triggered). Saves transcripts for audit.

Enables infinite conversations.

🛡️

Built-in Tools

File I/O (read, write, edit), bash execution with safety guards, todo tracking. Subagent support for parallel work.

🌊

Streaming

Real-time token delivery. Events: text_delta, tool_start, tool_result, done. WebSocket or REPL — same code path.

Tools

Write a Function → Get a Claude Tool

The ToolLoader converts your Python functions into Claude tool schemas. Type hints become input schemas. Docstrings become descriptions.

# tools/support_tools.py __tools__ = ['get_ticket_stats', 'search_tickets'] def get_ticket_stats( team: str, period: str = "last_week", priority: str = "all" ) -> str: """Get ticket statistics for a team. Args: team: Team name (e.g. "payments", "auth") period: Time period (default: last_week) priority: Filter by priority (P0-P3 or all) """ df = query_db( f"SELECT * FROM tickets WHERE ..." ) return df.to_json(orient="records")

What Claude sees — auto-generated, no manual schema writing:

// Auto-generated Claude tool schema { "name": "get_ticket_stats", "description": "Get ticket statistics...", "input_schema": { "type": "object", "properties": { "team": { "type": "string", "description": "Team name" }, "period": { "type": "string", "description": "Time period" }, "priority": { "type": "string", "description": "Filter by priority" } }, "required": ["team"] } }

✓ Type hints → JSON schema types

✓ Default values → optional params

✓ Docstring Args → param descriptions

✓ __tools__ list → selective export

Skills

Teach the Agent Domain Knowledge

# skills/support-ops/SKILL.md --- name: support-ops description: Support ticket analytics and SLA tracking --- ## ROLE & SCOPE You analyze support ticket data to help ops teams track SLAs, identify bottlenecks, and forecast staffing needs. ## AVAILABLE TOOLS - `get_ticket_stats` — ticket counts, SLA % - `search_tickets` — full-text search ## KEY TERMINOLOGY - SLA breach: response > 4h for P0/P1 - Resolution rate: tickets closed / opened - MTTR: mean time to resolve ## RULES - Always break down by priority tier - Show week-over-week trends by default - Flag any SLA breach rate > 5%

Layer 1: Always Visible

Skill name + description are injected into the system prompt on startup. The agent always knows what skills exist.

Skills available: - support-ops: Support ticket analytics - incident-mgmt: Incident response tracking

Layer 2: Loaded On-Demand

Full skill body is injected only when the agent calls load_skill("support-ops"). Saves tokens until the knowledge is actually needed.

Auto-Discovery

Drop a folder in skills/ with a SKILL.md — the SkillLoader finds it automatically. No config changes.

Frontend

Streaming Chat UI — Rebrand in One File

// web/project-config.ts (project-level override) export const projectOverrides = { name: "SupportBot", tagline: "AI-powered support analytics", appLogo: "/logo.png", pageTitle: "SupportBot — Ops Intelligence", disclaimer: "AI can make mistakes.", theme: { accent: "#6366f1", // only override what you need }, sampleQuestions: [ { icon: "AlertTriangle", text: "Show P0 SLA breaches this week" }, ] }
🎨

Override Config = Full Rebrand

Create web/project-config.ts with your overrides. The framework deep-merges with defaults — only specify what's different.

🌊

Real-time Streaming

WebSocket connection streams tokens as they arrive. Tool execution shown live with status indicators. Charts render inline.

💬

Conversation Persistence

Full chat history stored in DB. Sidebar shows past conversations. Resume any session.

🔐

Auth Built In

Azure AD SSO in production. Dev mode bypass with DEV_MODE=true. No auth code to write.
